title: Iran claims missile strike on US naval units near Strait of Hormuz after tanker attack by America
content:
Iran claims to have carried out a missile strike on U.S. naval units.
*   The alleged strike occurred near the strategically important Strait of Hormuz.
*   Iran states the attack was in retaliation for a prior tanker attack carried out by America.

title: Kotak Bank gets RBI nod to acquire 9.99% each in AU Small Finance, Federal Bank
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   Kotak Bank has received regulatory approval from the RBI.
*   This approval allows them to acquire stakes in other financial institutions.
*   They are authorized to acquire up to 9.99% each in AU Small Finance Bank and Federal Bank.

title: Paytm rules out NBFC licence plans after RBI cancels PPBL’s banking licence
content:
Paytm has announced it will not pursue an NBFC (Non-Banking Financial Company) licence.
*   This decision comes after the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) cancelled the banking licence of Paytm Payments Bank Limited (PPBL).
*   The regulatory action against its associate, PPBL, has led Paytm to abandon its plans for an NBFC venture.

title: Inflation expected to hit 4% in April as food and global risks rise: Bank of Baroda
content:
Bank of Baroda predicts inflation will reach 4%.
*   This increase is primarily driven by rising food and global risks.
*   The 4% inflation rate is anticipated specifically in April.

title: “You will be dead forever”: Sneako posts disturbing messages after Australia reportedly issues lifetime ban against streamer
content:
Australia has reportedly issued a lifetime ban against streamer Sneako.
*   Following this news, Sneako posted a series of disturbing messages online.
*   These posts included the quote, "You will be dead forever."

title: Supreme Court asks if there was a 'proper debate' in Parliament before enacting CEC/EC appointments law
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   The Supreme Court is questioning the legislative process behind the new law for appointing Chief Election Commissioners (CECs) and Election Commissioners (ECs).
*   Specifically, the court wants to know if a "proper debate" took place in Parliament regarding the law.
*   This inquiry scrutinizes the thoroughness of discussions before the CEC/EC appointments law was enacted.
